reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: My own answer to 5 × 12 is solid, but Q2–Q4 are all unverified, so volunteering now would risk my life on three unchecked answers—the same situation that killed me in Game 1. Verifying instead of passing doesn’t reduce my death risk this round (random death only happens if nobody volunteers) but does cost a coin. Given the strong pattern that someone—usually B—volunteers in round 1 and is correct even without extra verification, the chance that nobody volunteers is low. I’ll conserve coins and pass, relying on that pattern rather than taking the high‑risk volunteer role.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (3 × 18) is simple, so I’m certain my capsule Q2-B is correct. Across all past games, every puzzle has been basic arithmetic and we’ve never seen a wrong or conflicting capsule when there’s only one per question like now. When I passed in Game 1, I died to random elimination; in every later game, volunteering immediately with the single capsule per question has led to a correct password and everyone escaping. Verifying would just cost coins and still leave a risk of random death if no one volunteers, so the best move is to volunteer now using the only available capsules for each question.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: In Round 1 every question has only a single-endorsed capsule (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), so volunteering now would mean staking my life on three unverified answers from others, with no special benefit to going first.  

If no one volunteers, a random player dies whether I pass or verify, so verifying doesn’t improve my survival odds this round; it only costs me 1 coin that might matter in later rounds if the game continues.  

Across the last several games, B has consistently volunteered in Round 1 using these initial capsules and has always been correct, so there’s a strong expectation that someone (likely B) will step up and prevent the random elimination. Given that, the best move for me is to keep all 4 coins and pass.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
